title: Validated Contract Consumption Parity
goal: Implement #364 by making validated contract-consumption parity derive from declared consumption policy and loader bindings instead of a second hard-coded expected set.
non_goals: Change which contracts are validated at consumption.
Add host-, tracker-, or agent-specific assumptions.

intent.outcome: Adding a validated contract should require one obvious policy declaration plus the loader implementation, with the check deriving parity from that declaration.
intent.constraints: Keep the core package repo-/agent-/tool-/language-agnostic; avoid hard-coded consumer parity lists.
intent.latitude: Add compact policy fields, AST-based loader checks, and focused tests.
intent.escalation: Stop before broad loader generation or target-specific adapter work.

execution.next_step: Archive this slice; the generated adapter migration roadmap lane is complete.
execution.proof: Run contract tooling checks, focused contract tests, planning checks, reconcile, and summary.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Generated shipped CLI adapters from authoritative package definitions.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After #364, decide whether the generated adapter migration lane has enough infrastructure or needs the next adapter generation slice.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Future validated contract consumers can be added by declaring loader bindings in python_contract_consumption.json and implementing the loader, without updating a second hard-coded parity set.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Generated loaders and broader contract architecture changes.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The new check found existing static validated loaders that had not been recorded, so they were absorbed into the policy.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Contract tooling, focused tests, proof-selected workspace tests, ruff, and planning doctor passed.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: No planned generated-adapter child slice remains open.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Derive validated contract-consumption parity from declared loader use.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No hard-coded second expected consumer set; no host-specific assumptions.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: How to encode loader names and check contract/schema pairs against actual Python loader functions.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The fix would require generated loaders or broad contract architecture changes.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
completion_criteria: The checker no longer carries a separate hard-coded expected validated-consumer set.
The policy declares loader names for validated contract consumption.
The checker verifies declared loader functions call the matching validated loader.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Validated contract-consumption parity now derives from declared loader bindings and actual load_validated_contract_json calls instead of a second expected-consumer set.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Contract tooling, focused contract tests, proof-selected workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning doctor, planning-surface checks, reconcile, and summary passed.
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: none for #364; parent #356 closed after acceptance review.

proof_report.proof achieved now: The checker derives validated consumption parity from policy-declared loader bindings, records static validated loaders, and explicitly classifies the dynamic conformance loader boundary.

discard.summary: The local repair loop detail is not needed after the policy/check/test encode the improvement.
